AC: 5
STR: +3
Focus: Improved Healing I
WT: 6.0 Size: SMALL
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Slot: HEAD
AC: 5
STR: +3
Focus: Improved Healing I
WT: 6.0 Size: SMALL

Send a correction
Post Comment
Great for rangers
QuoteReply
# Dec 29 2003 at 4:36 AM Rating: Decent
animekenji
1,123 posts
Great item for rangers to keep in their backpacks until they get something with a better improved healing. That extra 20% may not seem like much to a pure healer class, but to us it's a godsend.
____________________________
If you were me and I were you, would we still be friends?

a little help
QuoteReply
# Oct 02 2002 at 4:02 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592255827250
11 posts
Any one think that a lvl 39 pal thats pretty strong can solo the west karana camp?
▲ Page top
Improved healing
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2002 at 6:42 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1595175338787
6 posts
This helm now (6/5-2002) have "Improved healing 1". Every healer below 24 (I think) want one now.
▲ Page top
RE: Improved healing
QuoteReply
# Jun 13 2002 at 12:11 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1593361240176
Scholar
26 posts
this is basicly the same as Zaharn's Coronet except the Zaharn's Coronet also has mana +25, and the Coronet also got the improved haling I.
▲ Page top
catchy
QuoteReply
# Oct 26 2001 at 9:32 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592251533705
17 posts
this item looks really cool when you were it
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 02 2001 at 11:23 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) how do i tell them i wanna buy this?
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 14 2001 at 7:36 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I like beer
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 08 2001 at 9:06 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) My level 6 Monk just bought this item cheap. It looks goofy like a banded helm with a pointy tip and shimmering little wings on the sides. A low weight monk helm would be better item because this has a +3-6=-3 weight carrying effect. However, the +3str does add to my Attack which offsets the weight. The +5AC is a definite boost and keeps me from dying so much.
Why does everything have to do with Monks?
QuoteReply
# Jun 25 2001 at 3:06 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592249252977
4 posts
Why does every item have a comment about it being either a) good for monks or b) bad for monks?

All of the other classes don't care !
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Oct 02 2001 at 1:52 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Yeah, especially this one - which is clearly and obviously one of the worst Monk helms in the game.
#Anonymous, Posted: Apr 30 2001 at 2:09 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) "My mage wears this item to increase str so as not to be overencumbered."
#Anonymous, Posted: Mar 02 2001 at 4:45 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Is this visible on most magicians?? Or more importantly, is it worth buying?
RE: Mages???
QuoteReply
# Apr 06 2001 at 2:04 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1593965894874
40 posts
It is visible as a helm on mages. Appears like a banded helm. Problem is that the Wt is 6.0, so the 3STR won't help you much when you're adding another 6 to your weight.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Mar 15 2001 at 8:03 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Yes, it has the same graphic as the runed circlet, a metal type helm. My mage wears this item to increase str so as not to be overencumbered. Int items can be found everywhere for every slot there is. The helm is a nice str adder...
RE: Mages???
QuoteReply
# Mar 06 2001 at 4:12 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591750104165
53 posts
Why would a mage even remotely desire this item? STR doesnt really help a mage or if it does you are doing something wrong.
▲ Page top
RE: Mages???
QuoteReply
# Jan 10 2003 at 7:25 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591771769340
Scholar
22 posts
Mages have a healing spell for their pet.
▲ Page top
RE: Mages???
QuoteReply
# Oct 21 2003 at 6:58 PM Rating: Decent
Valdon
674 posts
I dont think thats classified as a healing spell to be clear. That is a Re-Summon of the elements... Which I dont think is classified as such. It might be cool, but Id think it under something like Conjuration or Alteration. Possibly something like burning affliction might effect that? Id have to look. Id think a focus effect would work on that.
____________________________
· 'Valdon Gravedancer' Terris-Thule. Dwarven Cleric 34th circle.
· 'Junglebug Sugarpants' Terris-Thule. Halfling Druid. 52nd season.
· 'Shinook Eldergod' Terris-Thule. Troll Shaman of 32nd rite.
· 'Soultree Vixen' Terris-Thule. Woodelf Ranger of 34th season.
▲ Page top
RE: Mages???
QuoteReply
# Oct 26 2003 at 10:32 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593966395625
1 post
You are wrong, It is classified as a healing spell. All your renew element type spells are.


Nabren 55th lvl mage
▲ Page top
RE: Mages???
QuoteReply
# Apr 08 2001 at 9:49 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592246180773
5 posts
Especially at higher levels (though I've had my circlet of mist which has equivalent ac for the last 20 levels) str only helps to increase carry weight, and since this PoS weighs 6 lb.s anyhow... stick with circlet of mist, mages, at least until you start getting planar or velious quest helms.

Cueball
45 Mage
Brell
▲ Page top
Mages!!!
QuoteReply
# Mar 12 2001 at 8:49 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592245575947
13 posts
Obviously, th' lad's a wee bit skewed in 'is thinkin', else 'e wouldn't be worryin' if'n it shows up as coverin' 'is melon! But, in 'is defense, STR is indeed an attribute some like to increase. 'specially us....er,.....THEM loners. Y'know, the sort that finds their pet's conversation a bit more mature than, say, a n00b's. Then, m'friend, STR and AC are somethin' to take inta consideration when yer shoppin' 'round.
